Fig. 2: Training stage progression. Stage I trains only the modality-specific stems and adapters with the backbone frozen. Stage II introduces the neighborhood KL loss LA and populates the teacher queue while keeping the backbone frozen. Stage III unfreezes 50% of backbone blocks (25% for ViT-G) and rebalances loss weights to emphasize cross-modal consistency and spatial alignment.

Fig. 1: Overview of the SpectraDINO architecture. A frozen DINOv2 teacher provides target embeddings for RGB inputs. The student SpectraDINO processes both RGB and multispectral inputs through modality-specific stems into a shared ViT backbone augmented with per-block adapters and modality embeddings. All modalities share the same transformer weights: only the stems, adapters, and modality embeddings are modality-specific.
